# UC-22.15.43 · PLC CPU Load and Scan Cycle Degradation

## Description

Monitors embedded controller resource metrics to catch resource exhaustion from misconfiguration or attack precursors.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_perf and sourcetypes snmp:plc. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Poll at safe intervals; (2) baseline per process; (3) alert above threshold; (4) tie to recent downloads. Indexes required: `index=ot_perf`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snmp:plc`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SNMP from OT NMS or vendor API HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_perf` `sourcetype="snmp:plc"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Poll at safe intervals; (2) baseline per process; (3) alert above threshold; (4) tie to recent downloads.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_perf sourcetype="snmp:plc" earliest=-24h metric_name IN ("cpuUtil","scanCycleMs","taskOverload")
| timechart span=15m perc95(metric_value) by host, metric_name
| untable _time metric_series metric_value
| where metric_value>85 AND like(metric_series,"%cpuUtil%")
```

Understanding this SPL

**PLC CPU Load and Scan Cycle Degradation (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 6.2)** — Monitors embedded controller resource metrics to catch resource exhaustion from misconfiguration or attack precursors.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_perf` `sourcetype="snmp:plc"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SNMP from OT NMS or vendor API HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_perf; **sourcetype**: snmp:plc. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_perf, sourcetype="snmp:plc", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=15m** buckets with a separate series **by host, metric_name** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

• Filters the current rows with `where metric_value>85 AND like(metric_series,"%cpuUtil%")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT engineering record and session recording the control owner would use in an investigation. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table (worst hosts), Single value (breach minutes).

## SPL

```spl
index=ot_perf sourcetype="snmp:plc" earliest=-24h metric_name IN ("cpuUtil","scanCycleMs","taskOverload")
| timechart span=15m perc95(metric_value) by host, metric_name
| untable _time metric_series metric_value
| where metric_value>85 AND like(metric_series,"%cpuUtil%")
```

## Visualization

Time chart, Table (worst hosts), Single value (breach minutes).
